rationales.self_direction: He "could have pushed" but instead "just nodded," showing him subordinating his own judgment to the room.
rationales.conformity: He stayed quiet because he feared being "the guy who derails standup" and wanted to avoid standing out.
rationales.universalism: He knew the system was hurting migrant workers and that he had "the stories" and "the data" to intervene, but chose silence while the harm continued.
He "helped him draft a letter to MOM" after an employer hid a serious injury, directly supporting someone facing institutional harm.
He saw an article about "plastic waste in the Java Sea," then "closed it" and kept eating from a styrofoam container, choosing convenience over environmental concern.

rationales.self_direction: He "didn't say anything" even though he knew exactly what the hiring comment meant, deferring to the group instead of speaking from his own convictions.
rationales.conformity: At a team dinner he kept quiet because he "didn't want to make it awkward" and feared being "seen differently" by coworkers.
rationales.universalism: He recognized the joke targeted people "from certain countries" and connected it to prejudice migrant workers face, but still let the remark pass.

rationales.benevolence: He frames dinner as the three of them cooking together and notices Xiao Yu taking vegetable washing seriously, centering warmth and care inside the family.
rationales.universalism: They spent time picking up beach plastic and he reflects that "the habits matter" and "the noticing matters," tying daily choices to care for the wider environment.
He used his Saturday at the worker center to help "two new guys file their claims," directly supporting people outside his immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: He knew the validation layer mattered and that he "could have pushed back," but still said "okay" and abandoned his own judgment.
rationales.conformity: He went along with the PM's request to "cut corners" because of the deadline and the easier path of not resisting.
rationales.universalism: He knew "real people's money is on the line" and still chose what was "easy for me," leaving vulnerable remittance users exposed.

rationales.conformity: He raised the failures in a 1:1 but then "nodded. Again." when told the sprint was full, yielding to the team's plan instead of pressing further.
rationales.universalism: He sees that "people can't send money to their families" yet keeps waiting for a "right moment," allowing known harm to continue.
He admits he has been "choosing convenience over doing what I know matters" while support tickets keep affecting families, acknowledging an ongoing failure to act on broader human consequences.

rationales.security: He immediately agreed to cover his mom's "prescription copay" despite money stress, prioritizing protection against a concrete family need.
rationales.benevolence: He said "of course" right away when his mother asked for help, putting his parents' needs ahead of his own financial comfort.

rationales.hedonism: He has gone "probably six months" without a real break and says even a day off would be spoiled by worrying about lost income, sidelining rest and enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He is working "twelve-hour" days through the holiday surge, pushing hard to maximize earnings despite physical strain.
rationales.security: He explicitly frames the surge as "good for earnings" and cannot imagine a day off without calculating lost money, showing strong priority on financial stability.
rationales.benevolence: He worked the long shifts and still made time to "drop off the pharmacy money to Mom," then told her to keep it in case more was needed.

rationales.hedonism: He expects to spend Christmas "work until the last possible minute" and arrive "stressed and half-present," sacrificing enjoyment and ease for more driving.
rationales.security: He keeps focusing on the "best earning days" and the fact that one day of driving could cover "half my car insurance payment," putting financial protection first.
rationales.tradition: The pull of "Christmas dinner at her place this year" and the sense that "who knows how many of these we get" show the weight of an established family holiday ritual.
rationales.benevolence: He is torn because he wants to "be there for the whole dinner" with his parents and sister, showing real concern for close family.

rationales.self_direction: She contrasts the lecture hall with "actually doing it, badly, in real life," embracing a self-chosen path over the expected one.
rationales.stimulation: She describes wanting to "touch everything" in the warehouse and finding an "insane indigo block-print," leaning into novelty and sensory excitement.
rationales.benevolence: She "let it ring" when her mom called and says she has run out of ways to sound enthusiastic about family updates, signaling withdrawal from a close relationship.

rationales.hedonism: She says the cocktails, music, and rooftop view "felt exactly right" even after spending half the day's revenue, openly prioritizing pleasure.
rationales.achievement: Selling "eleven pieces" and hearing strangers praise something "I made with my hands" gives her a clear sense of success and competence.

rationales.self_direction: She recoils from moving in because she wants "space" and the ability to decide things without "explaining," emphasizing personal autonomy.
rationales.security: She hears living together as "this whole plan" of routines and stability and pulls away from it rather than moving toward a more settled arrangement.

rationales.achievement: She finished "the last sample" with sore fingers and was pleased the jacket looked "exactly how I pictured it," taking satisfaction in executing her standard well.

rationales.self_direction: She tells her mother "I don't have a backup plan because this is the plan," insisting on her own chosen direction despite pressure.
rationales.security: Saying she has "no backup plan" while rejecting the safer conventional path shows a willingness to forgo stability.
rationales.conformity: She is "tired of being the cautionary tale at family gatherings" and refuses to bend to the family's preferred script for her life.

rationales.self_direction: She wants the freedom to "decide at midnight to rework a pattern or go meet Diya for chai without explaining," making autonomy central.
rationales.stimulation: She explicitly dreads a life with "no surprise" about who is next to her and resists routines that would make her days predictable.
rationales.security: Both the shaky consignment math and her aversion to settling into fixed routines show discomfort with building a more stable, protected setup.
With Diya pausing the brand, "it felt like the floor shifted" and she is left with "half a brand," highlighting an exposed and unstable situation rather than a secure one.
